issue_title: shed_upload silent if missing .shed.yml include files
issue_body:
My reading of the code suggests that the default ``include`` value is ``**`` (all files next to the ``.shed.yml`` file). I have tried adding an explicit listing of files, e.g.

```
$ more ~/repositories/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list/.shed.yml
name: venn_list
owner: peterjc
homepage_url: https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list
remote_repository_url: https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list
description: Draw Venn Diagram (PDF) from lists, FASTA files, etc
long_description: |
  Draw Venn Diagrams for 1, 2 or 3 sets of identifiers as a PDF file.

  Can parse FASTA, FASTQ, SFF or tabular files (taking column one) for identifiers.
  This can be combined with the extensive tabular file filtering and manipulation
  tools within Galaxy to prepare the input data.

  Uses the R/Bioconductor package limma to draw the PDF file, called from Python with
  rpy.

  Uses Biopython to parse SFF files.
categories:
- Graphics
- Sequence Analysis
- Visualization
type: unrestricted
include:
- README.rst
- venn_list_with_typo.py
- venn_list.xml
- tool_dependencies.xml
```

This fails to complain about missing ``venn_list_with_typo.py``:

```
$ planemo shed_upload --tar_only  ~/repositories/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list 
cp /tmp/tmpx0zfjB shed_upload.tar.gz
$ tar -tzf /tmp/tmpx0zfjB
tool_dependencies.xml
README.rst
venn_list.xml
```

On the positive side, it did not include ``venn_list.py`` because it wasn't asked to (deliberate mismatch with ``venn_list_with_typo.py``).

I want this example to fail, complaining that ``venn_list_with_typo.py`` was listed in the includes, but not found. Ideally the error would list all named but missing files, but just the first missing file would be acceptable.

As an aside, would checking the include files exist also fall under ``planemo lint`` functionality for validating the ``.shed.yml``?

Since `SlugField` supports unicode since Django 1.9 (https://docs.djangoproject.com/es/1.9/ref/models/fields/#django.db.models.SlugField.allow_unicode), which is already supported by latest Oscar, we should probably think about proper unicode slugs support in the Oscar too.

Firstly, we could make optional conversion from unicode to ASCII in the slugify function - https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/master/src/oscar/core/utils.py#L41.

Also, perhaps there's no need in custom `oscar.models.fields.AutoSlugField`, since original one from `django_extensions` already support custom slugify function - https://github.com/django-extensions/django-extensions/blob/master/django_extensions/db/fields/__init__.py#L109.

Thanks to @pwalsh for the help in discovering this issue.

issue_title: Searching for node/nodejs in sandboxjs.py broken
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
Checking for the existence of nodejs or node should not throw an exception.

## Actual Behavior
The code that checks for the presence of node/nodejs does not catch the correct exception when a given binary is not present. It should just gracefully catch the exception an move on with its life.

## Proposed fix
Change line 81 of sandboxjs.py from:
```python
        except OSError as e:
            if e.errno == errno.ENOENT:
                pass
            else:
                raise
```
to:
```python
        except OSError as e:
            if e.errno == errno.ENOENT or e.errno == errno.EACCES:                                                                                                                                                          
                pass
            else:
                raise
```

issue_title: CKEditor getUrl cache issue - modify timestamp on deploy?
issue_body:
Hey there!

CKEditor's [`getUrl()` method](https://docs.ckeditor.com/ckeditor4/latest/api/CKEDITOR.html#method-getUrl) adds a so called timestamp to every link it processes. It's meant to help update cached static files when the lib gets updated, or a new bundle of the same version is downloaded from their site.

However the [timestamp](https://docs.ckeditor.com/ckeditor4/latest/api/CKEDITOR.html#property-timestamp) appears to hard-coded in CKEditor's JS. Which leads to the caching problem being even worse if one only updates some static files without redownloading/updating CKEditor. For example, if we're developing custom plugins and push changes to their JS and CSS from time to time - these files' cached versions don't get cleared.

My question is: is there a method in django-ckeditor to solve that? I can think of generating some unique value on each deploy and set the `CKEDITOR.timestamp` prop to that value (and have it empty on a dev environment). How do you overcome this issue?

@BibMartin correct me if I am wrong, but `GeoJsonStyle` is only for color scale, right?

PS: I am creating more examples in an effort to accumulate material to write the new version of the docs.

This isn't a bug, so much as a question for any grafanalib users who might be watching.

How would you feel about a jsonnet version of grafanalib?

Prompted by [recent announcement](https://blog.heptio.com/ksonnet-intro-43f6183a97a6) of [ksonnet](http://ksonnet.heptio.com/).

issue_title: possible bug in model for fp.fma
issue_body:
Hey,

I am not sure if the attached testcase should be ultimately SAT or UNSAT. However, I am reasonably sure that the model is bogus.

```
(set-logic QF_FP)
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-info :source |Florian Schanda|)
(set-info :smt-lib-version 2.5)
(set-info :category crafted)

(declare-const x Float32)
(declare-const y Float32)
(declare-const z Float32)

(define-const x2 Float64 ((_ to_fp 11 53) RNE x))
(define-const y2 Float64 ((_ to_fp 11 53) RNE y))
(define-const z2 Float64 ((_ to_fp 11 53) RNE z))

(define-const r Float32 (fp.fma RNE x y z))
(define-const r2 Float32
  ((_ to_fp 8 24) RNE (fp.add RNE (fp.mul RNE x2 y2) z2)))

(assert (not (= r r2)))
(check-sat)

(get-value (x y z))
(get-value (x2 y2 z2))
(get-value (r r2))
(get-value ((= r r2)))

(exit)
```

If I run this with the current master branch I get:
```
sat
((x (fp #b0 #x80 #b10111011101101010010011))
 (y (fp #b1 #xfe #b00000000010100101111000))
 (z (fp #b0 #xfe #b11111000100010011100101)))
((x2 (fp #b0 #b10000000000 #xbbb5260000000))
 (y2 (fp #b1 #b10001111110 #x0052f00000000))
 (z2 (fp #b0 #b10001111110 #xf889ca0000000)))
((r  (fp #b1 #xfe #b10000000000000000000001))
 (r2 (fp #b1 #xfe #b10000000000000000000001)))
(((= r r2) true))
```

In particular r and r2 seem quite equal in this model, but that is not what I asserted. (And yes, I did intend to use '=' and not 'fp.eq'.)

issue_title: A new official release is sorely needed..
issue_body:
The last official release of Z3 is now some ~1400 commits behind the master, according to https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/releases.

Would it be at all possible to have a new official release out?

(Users of tools built on top of Z3 usually get the "official release" instead of nightly ones, and get quite confused when they see discrepancies/non-working items upstream. There have been many great changes to Z3 since the 4.5.0 release; would be really nice to have an official version out..)

Hi,

lasagne.layers.batch_norm conveniently removes the bias term from the layer, as it is redundant with the batchnorms mean subtraction.

However, I recently read [here](http://arxiv.org/abs/1507.02672) (page 6), that in a similar way, for ReLUs the scaling in the Batchnorm is redundant:

> For example, the rectified linear unit does not need scaling, the linear activation function needs neither scaling nor bias, but softmax requires both.

First, I'm not totally sure if its true ( they have a funny nomenclature, and according to their formulas, they use scaling/bias slightly differently). I tried to do the math myself, but couldn't definitely figure out if its correct. Intuitively, it makes sense: You shift your input to zero mean; everything <0 gets squashed to 0 by the RelU; all >0 gets just the identity, and its irrelevant how you scale this.

Can we handle/remove this redundany somehow in batch_norm? Since the ReLU is the default nonlinearity for most (?) layers, this might change quite a bit

As already briefly discussed at issue #366, parsing the reader yaml files causes the pattern to change the path separator depending on the platform. This results in the `satpy.find_files_and_readers` function to never return any results.

The path separator in the yaml files are, by convention, specified as `"/"`, for example the SLSTR yaml:

`'{mission_id:3s}_SL_{processing_level:1s}_{datatype_id:_<6s}_{start_time:%Y%m%dT%H%M%S}_{end_time:%Y%m%dT%H%M%S}_{creation_time:%Y%m%dT%H%M%S}_{duration:4d}_{cycle:3d}_{relative_orbit:3d}_{frame:4d}_{centre:3s}_{mode:1s}_{timeliness:2s}_{collection:3s}.SEN3/{dataset_name}_radiance_an.nc'`

The `AbstractYAMLReader` splits this pattern on that same separator, but then uses `os.path.join` to join in back to a path. In doing this, the `os.path.join` will use the platform depending separator. So on Windows, the pattern shown above transforms to:

`'{mission_id:3s}_SL_{processing_level:1s}_{datatype_id:_<6s}_{start_time:%Y%m%dT%H%M%S}_{end_time:%Y%m%dT%H%M%S}_{creation_time:%Y%m%dT%H%M%S}_{duration:4d}_{cycle:3d}_{relative_orbit:3d}_{frame:4d}_{centre:3s}_{mode:1s}_{timeliness:2s}_{collection:3s}.SEN3\\{dataset_name}_radiance_an.nc'`

The code doing this is:
```            
file_patterns = [os.path.join(*pattern.split('/'))
                             for pattern in filetype_info['file_patterns']]
``` 
https://github.com/pytroll/satpy/blob/master/satpy/readers/yaml_reader.py#L99

When searching for files, the [`get_filebase` function](https://github.com/pytroll/satpy/blob/master/satpy/readers/yaml_reader.py#L66) splits on `"/"`, but this separator won't occur because of the `os.path.join` mentioned above. Hence the length of the split will always return 1, causing the function to only return the filename, whereas it should return the parent folder as well. 

The resulting [`fnmatch` attempt](https://github.com/pytroll/satpy/blob/master/satpy/readers/yaml_reader.py#L78) will always fail because its comparing:

`fnmatch('S1_radiance_an.nc', '???_SL_?_??????_????????T??????_????????T??????_????????T??????_????_???_???_????_???_?_??_???.SEN3\\*_radiance_an.nc')`

Personally I think that using `os.path.sep` in `get_filebase` should work, since the pattern is created/parsed by using `os.path.join`. On Windows it does work, I tested it for the OLCI L1 and SLSTR L1 readers. But @djhoese expressed some concerns about this:
https://github.com/pytroll/satpy/issues/366#issuecomment-404569222

I'm not familiar enough with SatPy's internals to understand the implications of such a change.

Presently, the function 'cwt' does not operate on complex input data.  It calls '_check_dtype,' which causes a downcast to float, discarding the imaginary part of the input data. It is relatively simple to add 'complex' to the list of allowed dtypes inside '_check_dtype';  I have done it on my local copy and it works just fine afterwards.

When computing dwt/wavedec/packet, the keyword "per" should refer to the "periodization" mode. 
It would make a backward compatibility with  the nigma/pywt version.

Example:

```python
from scipy.misc import ascent
img = ascent() # (512, 512) image
P1 = pywt.WaveletPacket2D(img, "sym2", mode="periodization", maxlevel=1)
P1.decompose()
P2 = pywt.WaveletPacket2D(img, "sym2", mode="per", maxlevel=1)
P2.decompose()

print("P1 mode: %s, appcoeff 1 shape: %s" % (P1.mode, str(P1.a.data.shape))) # (256, 256) as expected
print("P2 mode: %s, appcoeff 1 shape: %s" % (P2.mode, str(P2.a.data.shape))) # (257, 257)
```
It looks like the computation is performed with the symmetric extension mode in the second case.

issue_title: Dashboard, Bookmark and PinnedApplication models should use a ForeignKey to User
issue_body:
I have a custom user model which swaps the id field with a UUID field. I tried setting up the dashboard and got a DataError

### To reproduce
* Create a custom user model with id as uuid field
```
import uuid
from django.contrib.auth.models import AbstractBaseUser, PermissionsMixin
class CustomUser(AbstractBaseUser, PermissionsMixin):
    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
```

* Set `AUTH_USER_MODEL` to this model
* Try to access the dashboard

You will see an error similar to this
`(1264, "Out of range value for column 'user' at row 1")`

### To fix
replace any user fields with
`user = models.ForeignKey(settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL, verbose_name=_('user'))` 
then change any references of `user.pk` to `user`

I'm working through trying to emit headers that are as unprocessed as possible. aiohttp's response object has a raw_headers variable that's a list of 2ples (good) and the values are bytes, not str. While I could certainly decode the headers before calling warcio, and the charset iso-8859-1 is safe for round-tripping like that, I'm just wondering if taking str is a bad interface. issue_title: Filters for post processing doesn't apply
Windows 10 Version 1607 causes camera to crash
issue_body:
| Required Info  |   |
|---|---|
| Camera Model | SR300 | 
| Firmware Version |  1.15.00 /  1.17.00   | 
| Operating System & Version |  Windows 10 : version 1607	os build 14393.51  |
| Build System |  VS2015 | 

The librealses seems to have problems with the recent windows 10 Build (version 1607) release on the 2.8.2016 .

I experince some random crashes of the camera. I could not pin done the exact line causing the issue but would recommand not to update to the current build yet.

(In case someone experinces the same problem, there is an option to revert the system back to the latest build ( without reinstalling the system). Officialy not available after 10 days with the new build.

UPDATE:
I testet this issue on different maschines :
 1. Running old build of win 10 -> no problems with firmeware 1.15 /1.17
 2. Running current build of win 10 -> error while init camera and set parameters
    -> After reset to old build of win 10 -> no problems with firmeware 1.15 /1.17 anymore.
3. Running current old build of win 10 ->  no problems with firmeware 1.15 /1.17
   -> After installing current build ->  error while init camera and set parameters

So this problems seems to be connected to the current windows build.

The error seems to be thrown here [uvc.wmf line 564] (https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/master/src/uvc-wmf.cpp#L564)
when setting a F200/SR300 parameter.

`ks_control->KsProperty((PKSPROPERTY)&node, sizeof(KSP_NODE), data, len, nullptr)`
allways returns -2147467261 (HRESULT : E_POINTER Invalid pointer. ) which indicates an error.
Same program with previos build returns 0 (indicates no error).

If i do ignore those error some parameters are still beeing set to the camera. However it is not possible to check if it was executed correctly ( which usually results in a retry till it worked)

Is it possible, that some interfaces were changed at this point (windows side) ? 

SOLVED:

Problem was that 
`ks_control->KsProperty((PKSPROPERTY)&node, sizeof(KSP_NODE), data, len, nullptr)` does not accept nullpointer anymore. Changed to 

```
ULONG bytes_received = 0;
			check("IKsControl::KsProperty", ks_control->KsProperty((PKSPROPERTY)&node, sizeof(KSP_NODE), data, len, &bytes_received));
```

@montoyjh 

Can you put all matminer examples (e.g. ipynb notebooks) in this repo:

I recently compared the performance of `matminer` to `Magpie` for computing composition-based features. For ~250k entries, `matminer` requires 24 minutes to evaluate the same features that take `Magpie` 4 seconds. 

From a quick profiling run, it seems like a fair amount of the time (25%) is spent retrieving elemental properties and, [surprisingly](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/3035), computing the mode of a list. Digging further, the slow part of retrieving elemental properties is sorting the properties by atomic number and calling `Composition.get_el_amt_dict()`. My plan is to adjust the AbstractData API such that it is possible to avoid performing either of these operations.

I have opened this issue to see if anyone else has ideas for speeding up the code, and to let you know I am overhauling the AbstractData API. Let me know if I should make a public branch if you want to work on this together and we can avoid merge conflicts.

issue_title: Re-fetching Relay Node with multiple types
issue_body:
This is more of a question.

I have a set-up similar to below:

```
class DjangoObj(DjangoObjectType):
    class Meta:
        model = models.DjangoObj

class Obj(CustomObjectType):
    # ...
    django_objs = DjangoConnectionField(DjangoObj)

class ObjConnection(graphene.Connection):
    class Meta:
        node = Obj

class Query(graphene.ObjectType):
    node = graphene.relay.Node.Field()
    # The naming does not really matter here
    objs = graphene.relay.ConnectionField(ObjConnection, )
```

`CustomObjectType` is similar to `DjangoObjectType`. When re-fetching using a global ID returned by a result in `django_objs`, I hit a `Received incompatible instance` error (https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/94e2a5c49f74f302cd0a04e988202957335f6346/graphene_django/types.py#L101).

The issue is resolved by implementing `is_type_of` for `CustomObjectType`. But, if I add a `raise` similar to how `DjangoObjectType` does it, I receive an exception in `CustomObjectType` because the Django model is not compatible with my custom type.

The issue can again be resolved by adding to `Query`:

```
django_objs = DjangoConnectionField(DjangoObj)
```

What is going on internally? From the looks of it, we are going through all the possible types, and testing them with `is_type_of`, which would explain the aforementioned exception. But, if that is the case, perhaps we do not want to `raise Exception` in `types.py` ? We simply want to return `False` ?

Now with the latter solution of _registering the field_ in `Query`, I suppose it simply exposes the type, so something like `is_type_of` would be triggered early?

issue_title: Add a custom summary op for PR curves that takes raw precision, recall, TP, FP, TN, FN.
issue_body:
An internal team has a custom way of computing PR curves. For their use case, true positives are rare, so  the current PR curves plots produced by TensorBoard are noisy for them.

To solve this problem, they made a streaming metric that accumulates weighted (TP, FP, TN, FN) counts across a certain window of recent steps, which has a smoothing effect and makes the PR curves jump less from step to step.

To accommodate that, I am thinking about adding a raw_precision_recall_op method to the summary.py file for PR curves. I started this issue to get everyone's input on that.

@wchargin @jart @dandelionmane

issue_title: Image Preprocessing to finetune/predict using pre trained VGG model
issue_body:
The example at https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/blob/master/examples/images/vgg_network_finetuning.py shows how to fine tune the pre-trained VGG16 model.

However the trained model might have applied mean subtraction and Std normalization where the mean/STD values are calculated over entire training dataset.

if we need to fine-tune the model (or use it as-is for classification) shouldn't we have to apply the exact same preprocessing to new images?

The fine tuning example above applied Normalization to the input image but this will not match that over the entire pre-training dataset.

issue_title: Piping output to input of os command causes unhandled exception on Python 3.x
issue_body:
The output redirection feature where command output can be piped (|) as input to operating-system commands, as in "help | wc" is broken for Python 3.x but still works for Python 2.7.  It appears to be a str (unicode) vs bytes issue.

Not only is it broken, but it causes an unhandled exception.

When "debug" is set to True, this is the exception I got:
(Cmd) help | wc
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/toddleonhardt/src/cmd2/cmd2.py", line 872, in onecmd_plus_hooks
    stop = self.onecmd(statement)
  File "/Users/toddleonhardt/src/cmd2/cmd2.py", line 955, in onecmd
    stop = func(statement)
  File "/Users/toddleonhardt/src/cmd2/cmd2.py", line 543, in do_help
    cmd.Cmd.do_help(self, arg)
  File "/Users/toddleonhardt/anaconda/lib/python3.5/cmd.py", line 333, in do_help
    self.stdout.write("%s\n"%str(self.doc_leader))
TypeError: a bytes-like object is required, not 'str'

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/toddleonhardt/src/cmd2/cmd2.py", line 877, in onecmd_plus_hooks
    self.restore_output(statement)
  File "/Users/toddleonhardt/src/cmd2/cmd2.py", line 928, in restore_output
    self.kept_state.stdout.write(result or '')
TypeError: write() argument must be str, not bytes

issue_body:
Are you planing to use TensorFlow as a backend exclusively ?

Because when running a training with aetros on my server, using Keras with TensorFlow backend, Theano AND TensorFlow seems to run in parallel which leads me to this crash :

On this [issue](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/916), they say it may come from running both Theano and Tensorflow on GPU.

issue_title: Lib should be using latest secure, stable release of oauth2client?
issue_body:
Currently this project is pinned to require ``oauth2client==3.0.0``, however the most recent version is ``4.1.2``. There were security and stability improvements made in this package since ``3.0.0`` was released. Shouldn't this package be updated to support and use the newest version.

I appreciate that ``oauth2client`` is now deprecated and that going forward this project should favour using google-auth and oauthlib. But until time is found to update this project to use those two libraries, we should at the very least use the latest stable release of ``oauth2client``?

Thanks!

issue_title: Add a TTL for the protocol messages
issue_body:
### Problem

An initiator can try new routes at will, a new route is tried if the SecretRequest message is not received under a configurable timeout, when this happens the secret is discarded and makes the previous hashlock unusable.

Currently there is not way to inform the mediators that a given hashlock transfer was discarded by the initiator and that further tries to reach the target wont succeed.

### Solution

Add a time-to-live similar to TCP's TTL for the message to reach it's target.

Under a non byzantine operation:
1. For a direct transfer the TTL may be ignored, since the message is not mediated.
2. For a mediated transfer the TTL may be set to different values:
   1. If the initiator discards the secret after a timeout happens the TTL must be equal to this value.
   2. If the initiator does not discards the secret and tries more than one route simultaneously, then the TTL should be an approximation of the lock's expiration block.

Under byzantine behavior:
- The TTL might be set to a low value, which doesn't have negative effects
- The TTL is set to a value higher then the lock's expiration, this is the same as not using the TTL and doesn't have negative effects (if the lock has expired the TTL must be ignored).

### Considerations

The TTL is useful for the scenario `2.i` above.

The value of TTL does not interfere with the expiration time of a lock, meaning that if the TTL was not reached but the lock has expired the TTL is useless.

The TTL is only defined for the protocol messages, it has no implications for the Raiden signed messages, the only usage of this field is to improve the network efficiency, a mediator hop should not try to forward the payment because the initiator has discarded the secret and is trying a different route.

hashlocks act as transfer ids. 
they should be an optional parameter in the API `transfer` method. 

https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/raiden_service.py#L50
https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/transfermanager.py#L37

Addressing this will make our codebase more maintainable and improve [Better Code Hub](https://bettercodehub.com)'s **Couple Architecture Components Loosely** guideline rating! 👍 

Here's the gist of this guideline:
- **Definition** 📖 
Minimize the amount of interface code; that is, code in modules that are both called from and call modules of other components ("throughput"), and code in modules that are called from modules of other components ("incoming").
- **Why**❓ 
Having loose coupling between top-level components makes it easier to maintain them in isolation.
- **How** 🔧 
You can hide a component's implementation details through various means, e.g. using the "abstract factory" design pattern.

You can find more info about this guideline in [Building Maintainable Software](http://shop.oreilly.com/product/0636920049159.do). 📖

issue_title: Less violent way of closing player when stream ends
issue_body:
Currently streamlink uses SIGKILL to close the player when a stream ends. This prevents the player from doing its own cleanup. For example, mpv leaves DPMS/screensaver disabled because of this. I know there is --player-no-close option, but that has an unwanted side-effect of not closing the player immediately in some situations.

I suggest fixing it by using SIGTERM instead:
```diff
diff -bur streamlink-0.1.0-orig/src/streamlink_cli/output.py streamlink-0.1.0/src/streamlink_cli/output.py
--- streamlink-0.1.0-orig/src/streamlink_cli/output.py  2016-11-21 21:56:29.000000000 +0200
+++ streamlink-0.1.0/src/streamlink_cli/output.py       2016-12-08 22:08:23.000000000 +0200
@@ -161,7 +161,7 @@
 
         if self.kill:
             with ignored(Exception):
-                self.player.kill()
+                self.player.terminate()
         self.player.wait()
 
     def _write(self, data):
```

issue_title: Build a package and get it upstreamed into Debian
issue_body:
Currently livestreamer is available as a package which can be easily installed on Debian/Ubuntu, this is not the case for streamlink. If we want to get this packaged up and ready for the next release we need to move quickly. as @javiercantero mentions [here]https://github.com/chrippa/livestreamer/issues/1427#issuecomment-249653892) they'll be freezing the the release in the beginning of January. While I'm familiar with building RPMs and am okay at building debs I'm not very familiar with building debs that go into the upstream repo. If we have anyone who is an expert working on the project I would love to get your help with this. I don't think we'll be able to get this sort of coverage (http://docs.livestreamer.io/install.html#linux-and-bsd-packages), but having it be available for at least Debian and Ubuntu would be nice.

There is some existing stuff related to the builds that chrippa put in place (https://packages.debian.org/sid/video/livestreamer), but I think we'll have to do some clean up to get everything prepared.

 Is this something that we want to do? Or should we wait on a package till we've rebuilt the CLI, then hope we can get it in at a later date?

issue_title: on_conflict/upsert does not allow `pk` as a valid conflict target
issue_body:
```
class MyModel(psqlextra.Model):
     title = models.CharField(max_length=14)

MyMode.objects.on_conflict(['pk'], ConflictAction.UPDATE).insert(pk=0, title='great')
```

Raises the following exception:

```
django.core.exceptions.SuspiciousOperation: pk is not a valid conflict target, specify a list of column names, or tuples with column names and hstore key.
```

In Django, `pk` is a special keyword that always refers to the primary key of a model. `psqlextra/compiler.py` should also look at `model._meta.pk` when validating conflict targets.

issue_title: Provide a summary of retries at the end of the log
issue_body:
Discussing with @yadudoc about how exceptions from tasks which were retried should be presented to the user (whether or not they should be provided with exceptions from tasks which were retried), we decided that there are two main use cases:

1. The user is going to handle the exception
2. The user is going to use the exception for diagnostics

Case 1 only makes sense for the most recent exception. For case 2, the tracebacks are currently being logged. In addition, it would be good to provide a summary of retries per app and per site at the end of the log for diagnostics.

A really nice feature to have would be the ability of running some initialisation scripts when the localstack container is created, very similar to the `docker-entrypoint-initdb.d` folder used by the [postgres](https://github.com/docker-library/postgres/blob/c4cb816b3deccc29393b8278d1a5f144d2be26f1/9.6/Dockerfile#L43) and [mysql](https://github.com/docker-library/mysql/blob/fc3e856313423dc2d6a8d74cfd6b678582090fc7/5.7/Dockerfile#L22) Dockerfiles.

issue_title: Question: cherrypick or rebase commits from a merge request
issue_body:
Hi,

I have a webhook for merge requests, which evokes a script that creates a new merge request now to another target branch.

Lets say I have a source branch which was created off of master. This original source branch (which lives in a fork) has a merge request into the upstream master. Now I  would like to create a new merge request with the same commits as the first merge request onto another upstream target branch, let’s say next-major branch.

This works using the API and python-gitlab.

Only problem is that it is not that nice to use one source branch for merge requests into two different target branches. Reasons:

* There can be different merge conflicts for each merge, and solving this is awkward when there is one source branch
* Commits made to master which are inherited to the source branch and that are not present in the new target branch will be added to the list of commits in the new merge request. I would not like this, because it could be that these commits in fact should not be in the new target branch.

The better way to do this would therefore be to

* Create a new dev-branch (in fork) with parent being the forks copy of the upstream target branch 
* Rebase or in some way apply the commits from the original merge request onto this new dev-branch
* Create a merge request of this new dev-branch into the new target branch.

I can not seem to manage this with the API. There is a possibility to cherry-pick a merge request onto another branch, but when I try this, it only recognizes branches belonging to the target project. Not the fork project. Which is what I need, as all our merge requests come from forks, and the new source branch needs to be created in the fork.

Is there a way to do this that I have missed?


Thanks!
Maiken

After parsing text to get a tree, I was using transform() to modify certain tokens.  If I print the trees, I see the difference.  But when I use pretty() on the original and the modified tree, I get the same output.  

I've attached the Python demo.  (Note, I had to rename it with a .txt suffix because GitHub would not allow uploading a .py file.)
[demo.py.txt](https://github.com/lark-parser/lark/files/2005606/demo.py.txt)

Here's the output:
```
> python demo.py
Tree(start, [Tree(comment, [Token(ANONRE_0, '#begin blah blah blah')]), Tree(fancy, [Token(ANONRE_1, '@fancy text')]), Tree(plain, [Token(ANONRE_2, 'plain text {')]), Tree(plain, [Token(ANONRE_2, 'indented text ')]), Tree(plain, [Token(ANONRE_2, '}')]), Tree(plain, [Token(ANONRE_2, 'final text')]), Tree(comment, [Token(ANONRE_0, '#end')])])
start
  comment       #begin blah blah blah
  fancy @fancy text
  plain plain text {
  plain indented text
  plain }
  plain final text
  comment       #end

Tree(start, ['#begin blah blah blah', Token(ANONRE_1, u'~fancy text'), Token(ANONRE_2, u'plain text <i>'), Token(ANONRE_2, '__indented text '), Token(ANONRE_2, u'__<d>'), Token(ANONRE_2, 'final text'), '#end'])
start
  #begin blah blah blah
  @fancy text
  plain text {
  indented text
  }
  final text
  #end

```
As you can see in the plain print of the transformed tree, the token.value field contains the modified text.   
 (The '~' replaces '\@', '\<i>' replaces '{', '\__' shows indenting, etc.)  But when .pretty() is used on the transformed tree, the unmodified parsed values are displayed.

I ran this with the Python debugger, stepping through Lark's code.  This is what I think is causing it:
`Token` is a subclass of a `unicode` type.  **[utils.py:55, lexer.py:27]**

`Token.__repr__()` returns` (self.type, self.value)`  **[lexer.py:45]** and I see the modified token.value string in the plain print of the modified tree.

`Token.__str__()` is not overridden, so the base `unicode.__str__()` is invoked when `Tree._pretty()` executes `'%s' % (n,)` **[tree.py:31]** and so the original unicode string is fetched and formatted and returned instead of the modified `token.value` when using pretty() on the modified tree.

I'm going to attempt a few hacks to Lark to see what happens:
1.  add __str__() to the Token class to return self.value 
2.  modify the superclass's unicode text as well as self.value of Token in derived transformer class

issue_title: Different encoding with and without speedups
issue_body:
Hi,

we just ran into an issue with the encodings when speedups are enabled and when they are not.
When speedups aren't enabled a JSON string is always returned as a unicode python string.
With speedups enabled it depends on the JSON input.

With speedups:
```python
type(simplejson.loads('"abc"')) = "str"
type(simplejson.loads(u'"abc"')) = "unicode"
type(simplejson.loads(u'"abc"'.encode("utf-8"))) = "str"
type(simplejson.loads(u'"\xf6"'.encode("utf-8"))) = "unicode"
```

The same without speedups results in unicode all the time.
